instructions.instruction: Use this skill when **designing, implementing, or reviewing GraphQL APIs** in a Rails application with the `graphql-ruby` gem.
instructions.original_snippets: Use this skill when **designing, implementing, or reviewing GraphQL APIs** in a Rails application with the `graphql-ruby` gem.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger implement-graphql.

instructions.instruction: Before shipping a resolver/mutation slice, ALL of the following must be true (details in linked sections; do not duplicate checks in prose here):
instructions.original_snippets: Before shipping a resolver/mutation slice, ALL of the following must be true (details in linked sections; do not duplicate checks in prose here):

instructions.instruction: TESTING.md: specs in `spec/graphql/` use `AppSchema.execute` — **ALL spec files** (resolver specs AND mutation specs). Never use HTTP controller dispatch for GraphQL specs.
instructions.original_snippets: TESTING.md: specs in `spec/graphql/` use `AppSchema.execute` — **ALL spec files** (resolver specs AND mutation specs). Never use HTTP controller dispatch for GraphQL specs.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger implement-graphql.

instructions.instruction: SPEC:       Write failing spec (happy path + auth + validation error case) — see TESTING.md

instructions.instruction: FINAL CHECK: Verify every HARD-GATE item above against the code you wrote — all 8 must be true
instructions.original_snippets: FINAL CHECK: Verify every HARD-GATE item above against the code you wrote — all 8 must be true

instructions.instruction: Prefer **dedicated resolver classes** over inline field blocks for non-trivial logic.
instructions.original_snippets: Prefer **dedicated resolver classes** over inline field blocks for non-trivial logic.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger implement-graphql.

instructions.instruction: **FORBIDDEN:** Never call `object.association` directly and never use `.includes` on the scope — every association load MUST go through the dataloader (graphql-ruby 1.12+). This applies both in type field definitions and in list resolvers:
instructions.original_snippets: **FORBIDDEN:** Never call `object.association` directly and never use `.includes` on the scope — every association load MUST go through the dataloader (graphql-ruby 1.12+). This applies both in type field definitions and in list resolvers:
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger implement-graphql.

instructions.instruction: List resolvers must also prime the dataloader for each association the returned records will expose:
instructions.original_snippets: List resolvers must also prime the dataloader for each association the returned records will expose:
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger implement-graphql.

instructions.instruction: Write `description:` inline on **every** field in every type — no field left undescribed:
